title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Emma Crickenshaw meets with Inspector Cladock to discuss a telegram she received from a woman named Matilda, who may be the widow of Emma's late brother, Edmund. Emma recalls that the telegram stated Matilda was returning to France around Christmas. While Emma hopes the letter is genuine and that Edmund left an heir, her father and brothers suspect it is a premeditated extortion attempt. Her brother-in-law, Brian, believes the letter is real, but her brother Harold suggests it could be a fraud. Cladock notes the suspicious timing of Matilda's sudden return to Paris and her subsequent silence after Emma replied. He also considers the legal implications regarding Edmund's potential heir and the value of Rutherford Manor.

Cladock concludes that the woman in the coffin and the woman who wrote the letter are likely unrelated. He instructs Detective Inspector Weatherall to travel to 126 Rue de Crésange in Paris to investigate if a woman claiming to be Mrs. Crickenshaw received mail there in late December. Later, Cladock receives a telegram from the Paris Police Headquarters stating that the information corresponds to Anna Stravinsky of the Mariinsky Ballet. Believing the Martina Crickenshaw case is nearing resolution, Cladock decides to take a ferry to Paris.

Meanwhile, at the Crickenshaw residence, Emma invites Miss Maple and her niece, Lucie Ainsworth, for tea. Harold and Cecil express skepticism toward Lucie; Cecil questions her trustworthiness due to her cleverness. Harold criticizes Emma for suggesting the corpse might be Edmund's French girlfriend, arguing that such claims make the family appear suspicious to the police.
